new_claims_value: Reduces memory usage by 26-54% (peak tokens) while preserving task performance; preserves >95% of accuracy when distilled into smaller compressors; up to 46% gain for smaller LMs as long-horizon agents. Headline: 54.5% peak-token reduction on 8-objective QA while surpassing uncompressed EM/F1; baselines: No-Compression, FIFO, Retrieval, LLMLingua, naive Prompting; primary datasets: AppWorld, OfficeBench, 8-objective QA.

Universal cross-framework agent knowledge base with disagreement gate ensuring retrieved knowledge enhances reasoning. Headline: smolagents +18.7pp at pass@3 (55.2%->73.9%) and OpenHands +4.0pp on SWE-bench (24.3%->28.3%); baseline: same frameworks without Agent KB retrieval; primary datasets: GAIA, Humanity's Last Exam, GPQA, SWE-bench.

Open agentic framework using computers like a human via experience-augmented hierarchical planning + Agent-Computer Interface (ACI). Headline: 20.58% success rate vs. 11.21% baseline on OSWorld (83.6% relative improvement, +9.37pp absolute); baseline: GPT-4o vanilla OSWorld agent; primary datasets: OSWorld (369 Ubuntu tasks) and WindowsAgentArena.

Induces commonly-reused workflows from past experiences and applies them in offline (training) and online (test-time) modes. Headline: +24.6% on Mind2Web and +51.1% relative success-rate on WebArena over baseline; baseline: BrowserGym autonomous agent without human-annotated workflows; primary datasets: Mind2Web and WebArena (812 tasks across 5 websites).

Self-evolving agent system: self-questioning for task generation, self-navigating for experience reuse, self-attributing for sample efficiency. Headline: superior task-goal completion (avg@8/best@8) over Vanilla GRPO baseline using substantially fewer parameters; baseline: Vanilla GRPO RL without experience guidance/process rewards; primary datasets: AppWorld and BFCL-v3 (multi-turn split).

Proactive context management via folding inspired by retrospective consolidation; multi-scale granular condensation to deep consolidation. Headline: AgentFold-30B-A3B reaches 36.2% on BrowseComp and 47.3% on BrowseComp-ZH, surpassing DeepSeek-V3.1-671B (30.0% on BrowseComp) and OpenAI o4-mini; baselines: DeepSeek-V3.1-671B, GLM-4.5-355B, OpenAI o4-mini; primary datasets: BrowseComp, BrowseComp-ZH, WideSearch, GAIA.

Agent-controlled memory operations as tool-based actions for long/short-term memory; trained via three-stage progressive RL with step-wise GRPO. Headline: AgeMem reaches 54.31% avg on Qwen3-4B-Instruct, +23.52% over best baseline (Mem0/A-Mem) and +49.59% over no-memory; baselines: Mem0, A-Mem, LangMem, AgeMem-noRL ablation; primary datasets: ALFWorld, SciWorld, PDDL, BabyAI, HotpotQA.

Plan template extraction from completed agent runs with keyword retrieval and lightweight model adaptation. Headline: 50.31% serving-cost reduction and 27.28% latency reduction on average while preserving 96.61% of optimal performance; baselines: Accuracy-Optimal (large planner LM, no caching) and Semantic Caching; primary datasets: FinanceBench, TabMWP, QASPER, AIME 2024/2025, GAIA.

Lifelong personalization of LLMs continuously adapting to diverse user profiles; releases code and data for benchmarking lifelong personalized LLM systems. Headline: Persona Learning (k=3) reaches 8.29 helpfulness and 7.63 personalization, approaching the Golden Persona upper bound (8.34, 7.78); baseline: No-Persona (7.96, 7.35); primary dataset: PersonaBench (200 personas, 10 common + 10 persona-specific scenes each, 6,000+ data points).

Sparse attention mechanism using Hopfield-energy associative memory for token reconstruction; CVPR 2025. Headline: outperforms SOTA sparse-Transformer Coordination method with significantly fewer parameters and attention layers (no specific headline metric in abstract); baseline: Coordination and other sparse-Transformer baselines; primary dataset: Sort-of-CLEVR.

Generalist agent with minimal predefinition and maximal self-evolution via MCP generation. Headline: 75.15% pass@1 and 87.27% pass@3 on GAIA validation; 74.00% on Mathvista, 52.00% on PathVQA; baseline: complex agent systems with greater predefinition; primary datasets: GAIA, Mathvista, PathVQA.

Self-evolution from generalist to domain expert via MCP synthesis, abstraction, and curation into MCP Box. Headline: 83.03% pass@1 and 89.09% pass@3 on GAIA with ~15% reduction in mean tokens per example, vs. 75.15% pass@1 for the original Alita master; baseline: original Alita master without specialized MCP box; primary datasets: GAIA, PathVQA, Humanity's Last Exam.

Locating-then-editing parametric edits via null-space projection of perturbations preserving original knowledge. Headline: boosts existing methods by 36.7% on average across LLaMA3, GPT2-XL, GPT-J (32.85% Efficacy and 30.60% Generalization on LLaMA3); baselines: MEMIT, ROME, RECT, PRUNE, MEND, InstructEdit, plus Fine-Tuning; primary datasets: Counterfact and ZsRE for sequential editing, with GLUE for general-capability checks.

LLM agent builds and maintains memory graph integrating semantic and episodic information during exploration; outperforms memory baselines on text games and competitive on multi-hop QA. Headline: markedly outperforms established memory methods and strong RL baselines (no specific headline metric in abstract); baselines: memory methods and RL baselines; primary dataset: interactive text-game environments and multi-hop QA.

LLM agent builds and maintains memory graph integrating semantic and episodic information during exploration. Headline: markedly outperforms established memory methods and strong RL baselines, competitive with dedicated KG-based methods (no specific headline metric in abstract); baselines: memory methods and RL baselines; primary dataset: interactive text-game environments and multi-hop QA.

Associative recurrent memory transformer for long sequences. Headline: 79.9% accuracy on single-fact questions over 50M tokens on BABILong, surpassing RMT (76.4% at 10M) and Mamba (saturates near 500K); baselines: RMT and Mamba; primary dataset: BABILong multi-task long-context benchmark with associative-retrieval QA1-5 tasks.

Long-context architecture optimizing recall via test-time memory. Headline: +80% accuracy at 10M-token BABILong vs. Transformers and recent linear-recurrent baselines; baselines: Transformers and linear-recurrent models; primary dataset: BABILong (plus language modeling, common-sense reasoning, recall-intensive long-context tasks).

Direct browser interaction via Playwright with predefined actions and explicit memory of key conclusions across steps. Headline: BrowserAgent-RFT averages 0.484 LLM-judge score across six benchmarks vs. Search-R1-Instruct's 0.348 (~20% relative improvement on multi-hop QA); baseline: Search-R1-Instruct; primary datasets: NQ, PopQA, HotpotQA, 2WikiMultiHopQA, Musique, Bamboogle.

Stores high-level thought-templates distilled from problem-solving; buffer-manager dynamically updates the meta-buffer at 12% of the compute of competing methods. Headline: +11% on Game of 24, +20% on Geometric Shapes, +51% on Checkmate-in-One over previous SOTA; baseline: previous SOTA prompting/reasoning methods; primary dataset: 10 reasoning-intensive tasks including Game of 24, Geometric Shapes, Checkmate-in-One.

Constructivist Agentic Memory (Piaget-inspired): structured schemata, flexible assimilation, dynamic accommodation; incremental overlapping clustering balances performance and efficiency. Headline: avg +3.0% across all metrics over the best baselines RAPTOR and GraphRAG; baselines: RAPTOR and GraphRAG; primary datasets: NovelQA, QMSum, FABLES, MultiHop-RAG, ODSum-Story, ODSum-Meeting.

Cognitive psychology-inspired memory module for embodied agents. Headline: 85.8% success on ALFWorld and 56.0% on ScienceWorld, surpassing SOTA online LLM-centric approaches; baselines: SOTA online LLM-centric approaches; primary datasets: ALFWorld, ScienceWorld.

Comprehensive survey of continual learning of LLMs covering vertical (general->specific) and horizontal (across time/domains) continuity; structured around Continual Pre-Training, Domain-Adaptive Pre-training, and Continual Fine-Tuning. Conceptual / survey paper — no headline metric.

Collaborative multi-agent framework for Windows UI automation; scenario-aware Task Scheduler decomposes tasks; per-agent memory units for self-evolution; interactive backtracking for human intervention. Headline: 31.89% average score on GAIA, outperforming simulated-interaction baselines (DynaSaur 38.21%, Sibyl 34.55%, HF Agents 33.22%, MMAC 25.91%, raw GPT-4o 13.98%); baselines: DynaSaur, Sibyl, HF Agents, MMAC, GPT-4o; primary dataset: GAIA (466 tasks).

Cognitive-inspired iterative retrieval with dynamic memory workspace; impasse detection -> probing queries -> evidence retrieval -> memory integration. Headline: up to 11% relative improvement over the strongest RAG baseline; baseline: strong RAG baselines; primary datasets: four challenging long-context narrative benchmarks (200K+ tokens).

Compresses past memories for long-range sequence learning; introduces PG-19 benchmark; effective for speech and RL memory. Headline: 17.1 perplexity on WikiText-103 and 0.97 bpc on Enwik8 (SOTA at publication); baseline: Transformer-XL and prior LM SOTA; primary datasets: WikiText-103, Enwik8, PG-19.

Disentangles abstract tool creation and concrete decision execution; LLMs autonomously generate custom tools using documentation and code. Headline: 59.7% on MATH, 94.7% on TabMWP, 75.5% on the new Creation Challenge; baselines: chain-of-thought, program-of-thought (with/without rectification), tool-use (WolframAlpha API); primary datasets: MATH, TabMWP, and the introduced Creation Challenge (~2K problems).

Dynamic Structured Memory + Reasoning Tree using OWL-compliant KG over conversation history. Headline: dialogue consistency (DER) improved by over 48% and GPT score by up to 10.1% with Qwen-8B; baselines: GPT-4o (8.20 GPT score, 20.94% DER), MemoryBank (8.30, 23.88%), RAG, agentic working memories; primary dataset: MT-Bench-101 (13 task categories).

Self-improving AI iteratively modifies own code, validated through coding benchmarks; archive of generated agents. Headline: SWE-bench 20.0%->50.0% and Polyglot 14.2%->30.7% after 80 self-improvement iterations; baselines: base coding agent (no self-improvement) and DGM-without-self-improvement ablation; primary datasets: SWE-bench Verified (subsets of 10/60/200) and Polyglot (subsets of 10/50).

End-to-end deep reasoning agent integrating thinking, tool discovery, action execution; autonomous memory folding into episodic/working/tool memory; ToolPO RL training. Headline: DeepAgent-32B-RL reaches 89.0% on TMDB and 75.4% on Spotify, vs. best 32B baselines at 55.0% and 52.6%; baselines: CodeAct and ReAct workflow-based methods; primary datasets: ToolBench, API-Bank, TMDB, Spotify, ToolHop, ALFWorld, WebShop, GAIA, HLE.

World-model RL agent diffusing trajectories; "DIffusion As a Model Of eNvironment Dreams". Headline: mean human-normalized score of 1.46 on Atari-100k — a new best for agents trained entirely within a world model; baseline: prior world-model-based agents (e.g. DreamerV3); primary dataset: Atari-100k benchmark.

Trains a single Transformer to map queries directly to docids, bypassing external indexes. Headline: significantly outperforms dual-encoder models and beats BM25 in zero-shot setup (no specific headline percent in abstract); baselines: dual encoders and BM25; primary datasets: Natural Questions retrieval (and zero-shot eval).

First agent to obtain Minecraft diamonds purely from a standard offline dataset. Headline: outperforms previous world models "by a large margin" with no specific quantitative metric in abstract; baseline: previous world models; primary dataset: Minecraft offline-data diamond-collection challenge from raw pixels.

General world-model RL with single configuration across 150+ tasks; first algorithm to collect Minecraft diamonds from scratch without human data or curricula. Headline: collects diamonds in Minecraft from scratch (no specific headline percent in abstract); baselines: specialized methods per task; primary dataset: 150+ tasks including Minecraft.
Dual-process agent (Type-1 fast + Type-2 deliberative) with continual context refinement. Source URL returned 303 redirect — no specific headline metric/baseline/dataset extractable from MDPI page.

Persistent evolving memory of strategies, code snippets, problem-solving insights stored as concise transferable snippets. Headline: Claude 3.5 Sonnet AIME-2024 23.3%->50.0% (more than doubled) and GPT-4o Game-of-24 10%->99% under DC-RS; baseline: standard prompting without memory retention; primary datasets: AIME 2024/2025, Game of 24, equation balancing, GPQA-Diamond, MMLU-Pro (Engineering and Physics).

Mixture-of-LoRA for lifelong model editing with router network and continuous data-adapter mapping; AAAI-25. Headline: 96.08 generalization on ZsRE with GPT2-XL, outperforming 8 baselines by over 10%; baselines: GRACE and MELO (plus 6 others); primary datasets: ZsRE and CounterFact (1000 sequential edits with semantic rephrasings).

Human episodic memory inspired LLM; events segmented online via Bayesian surprise + graph-theoretic refinement; retrieval across 10M tokens. Headline: 100% accuracy on Passkey.Retrieval up to 10.2M tokens; outperforms InfLLM on 80% of LongBench task groups (up to +40% on retrieval); baselines: InfLLM (SOTA KV-retrieval), RAG with NV-Embed-v2, full-context LLMs; primary datasets: LongBench and ∞-Bench.

Efficient Memory-Augmented Transformer with key-value memory for QA. Headline: T5-base with EMAT improves 25.8 -> 44.3 EM on Natural Questions; baseline: parametric T5-base; primary datasets: Natural Questions, WizardOfWikipedia, ELI5.

Episodic-control method for cooperative multi-agent RL using semantic memory embeddings and desirability-based incentive rewards. Headline: marked convergence acceleration on super-hard SMAC maps over EMC/QMIX/QPLEX/CDS (no specific headline percent in abstract); baselines: EMC, QMIX, QPLEX, CDS; primary datasets: StarCraft II Multi-Agent Challenge (SMAC) and Google Research Football.

row_name: Episodic Memory Is the Missing Piece
new_claims_value: Position paper arguing episodic memory is the missing capability in current LLM agents; defines properties and design space rather than reporting empirical results. No headline metric in abstract.

Foundational continual-learning method analogous to synaptic consolidation; selectively slows learning on weights important for prior tasks. Headline: enables a single network to learn many tasks sequentially without forgetting (no specific quantitative headline in abstract); baseline: SGD without consolidation; primary datasets: permuted-MNIST and several Atari 2600 games.

LLM agent extracts knowledge as natural language from training tasks without parameter updates; in-context learning over accumulated experiences; AAAI 2024. Headline: 59% success on ALFWorld, beating ReAct (40%) and matching/exceeding Reflexion without task reattempts; baselines: ReAct and Act agents; primary datasets: HotpotQA (100 val), ALFWorld (134 solvable), WebShop (100), FEVER.

LLM multi-agent framework for financial decisions with manager-analyst hierarchy and conceptual verbal reinforcement; episodic self-critique; selective propagation reduces communication. Headline: 82.871% cumulative return on TSLA with 1.972 Sharpe ratio vs. Buy-and-Hold 6.425%; baselines: Buy-and-Hold, DRL agents (A2C, PPO, DQN), LLM-based FinGPT/FinMem/FinAgent; primary dataset: 8 stocks (TSLA, AMZN, NIO, MSFT, AAPL, GOOG, NFLX, COIN) Jan 2022 - Jun 2023 with prices/news/10-Q/10-K/earnings audio.

Gradient-free continual evolution of LLM agents via structured experience library through reflection on success/failure; identifies scaling laws of experiential growth. Headline: AIME25 +23% (40%->63.3% for Claude-Sonnet-4), USPTO50k +10%, ProteinGym +14%; baseline: vanilla LLM without experience library; primary datasets: AIME25, USPTO50k, ProteinGym.

Forgetting-curve-inspired memory replay for continual LLM learning; model-centric notion of time via optimizer update magnitude; intensity-aware regularization. Headline: 72.9% Overall Performance and -4.7% Backward Transfer on Standard CL Benchmark with Qwen3-0.6B, outperforming VBM by 1.2% OP and 0.9% BWT; baseline: VBM (Ebbinghaus-inspired replay scheduling); primary datasets: Standard CL Benchmark, Long Sequence Benchmark, SuperNI Benchmark.

Hierarchical agentic memory for multi-agent systems with three-tier graph (insight/query/interaction); bi-directional traversal. Headline: +20.89% embodied action success and +10.12% knowledge-QA accuracy over prevailing MAS memory mechanisms; baselines: prevailing MAS memory mechanisms; primary datasets: 5 benchmarks across 3 LLM backbones and 3 MAS frameworks.

Generic agentic memory framework for long-horizon LLM tasks. Headline: 64.07 F1 on HotpotQA-56K, vs. Mem0 (30.12) and MemoryOS (24.58); baselines: A-Mem, Mem0, MemoryOS, LightMem, long-LLM, RAG; primary datasets: LoCoMo, HotpotQA, RULER, NarrativeQA.

Park et al. simulacra of human behavior in a sandbox town with 25 agents emergent social dynamics. Headline: case-study of believable behavior (Valentine's Day party emergence) — no quantitative headline in abstract; baseline: ablations of memory/reflection components; primary dataset: custom Smallville sandbox (25 agents in The Sims-style environment).

Generative interactive environment trained unsupervised on 11B parameters from internet videos; learns latent action space without ground-truth actions. Headline: FVD 40.1 on Platformers and Δ_t PSNR of 1.91 for action controllability; baselines: oracle behavioral cloning, random agent, ViT tokenizer (FVD 114.5), C-ViViT (FVD 272.7); primary datasets: Platformers (6.8M 16-second clips, 30K hours, 2D platformers); RT1 robotics dataset for transfer.

Heavy-Hitter Oracle for KV-cache eviction balancing recent and high-impact tokens. Headline: up to 29x throughput on OPT-6.7B/OPT-30B with 20% heavy hitters retained, 1.9x latency reduction; baselines: DeepSpeed Zero-Inference, Hugging Face Accelerate, FlexGen; primary datasets: OPT/LLaMA/GPT-NeoX evaluated on HELM and lm-eval-harness (COPA, MathQA, OpenBookQA, PiQA, RTE, Winogrande, XSUM, CNN/Daily Mail), AlpacaEval, MT-Bench.
Hierarchical adaptive memory inference for sequential decision making. Source URL (Nature Sci. Reports) returned 303 redirect; no headline metric/baseline/dataset extractable from preview.

Heterogeneous-language memory for long-conversation LLMs. Headline: superior performance across four question categories on LoCoMo with significantly fewer context tokens (no specific headline percent in abstract); baseline: existing memory systems using unstructured embedding vectors via semantic similarity; primary dataset: LoCoMo.
Forgetting-curve-inspired LLM memory model. Source URL behind ACM paywall (403); no headline metric/baseline/dataset extractable.

Hierarchical Hindsight Reflection separates high-level planning memory from low-level execution memory; refined continuously through distillation. Headline: 75.9% on AlfWorld and 80.5% on PDDLGame, +3.5% and +8.3% relative over ExpeL; baseline: ExpeL; primary datasets: AlfWorld and PDDLGame.

Image-based Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for self-supervised pretraining. Headline: ViT-Huge/14 trained on ImageNet in <72 hours on 16 A100 GPUs (no explicit comparison metric in abstract); baseline: prior self-supervised image pretraining; primary dataset: ImageNet.

Agent Skill Induction (ASI) on the fly via program-based skills with verification. Headline: 40.4% success on WebArena, vs. vanilla baseline 32.7% (+7.7pp) and AWM text-skill 36.3% (+4.1pp), with 15.3% fewer steps; baselines: vanilla Claude agent and AWM (text-skill counterpart); primary dataset: WebArena (812 examples across 5 web domains).

Bounded-memory streaming attention with infinite context in principle. Headline: 9.67 ppl on PG19 with 114x memory compression vs. Memorizing Transformer 11.37 and Transformer-XL 11.88; solves 1M-token passkey retrieval after 5K-sequence fine-tune; baselines: Memorizing Transformer, Transformer-XL; primary datasets: PG19, ArXiv-math, 1M passkey-retrieval, 500K BookSum.

Iterative paradigm for long-horizon research agents using interaction scaling and evolving report as persistent memory; ICLR 2026. Headline: +14.5pp avg over six benchmarks; +19.2pp over ReAct on frontier models; gains 3.5%->42.5% extending to 2048 interactions; baselines: Search-o1, WebThinker, WebDancer, WebSailor; primary datasets: HLE, BrowseComp, BrowseComp-zh, GAIA, Xbench-DeepSearch, SEAL-0.

Open-world Minecraft agent combining multimodal LM with memory of pre-trained knowledge and game survival; completes 200+ tasks. Headline: 12.5% on ObtainDiamondPickaxe in 60 minutes — 5x SOTA reliability (vs. VPT at 2.5% in 20min, DEPS ~2.42%); baselines: GPT-based planning, ReAct, Inner Monologue, DEPS, VPT; primary dataset: 200+ Minecraft Universe Benchmark tasks across 11 task groups.

Real-time LLM personalization without parameter updates by extracting personalized factual triples and optimizing user-specific KGs; reduces compute by avoiding back-prop. Headline: 39%-61% efficacy improvement on Llama3-8B over six baselines; baselines: Fine-tuning, ROME, Knowledge Editing, Knowledge Neurons, MEND; primary datasets: CounterFact and CounterFactExtension.

Episodic memory module attached to a frozen LLM for fast knowledge editing without retraining. Headline: 8-10x speed-ups vs. competitive baselines with comparable accuracy on fact-editing; baselines: ROME, MEMIT, MEND, IKE on sequential editing; primary datasets: multiple fact-editing benchmarks (sequential setup).

Human-demonstration-based mobile GUI agent with DemoParser/KnowSeeker/ActExecutor; LearnGUI dataset. Headline: Gemini-1.5-Pro 19.3%->51.7% with single demo (+198.9% relative); UI-TARS-7B-SFT 18.1%->32.8% online (+14.7%); baselines: zero-shot inference of same models; SPHINX-GUI Agent (67.2% on AMEX); primary datasets: LearnGUI-Offline (2,252 tasks across 9 apps) and LearnGUI-Online (101 tasks across 20 apps).

Modular procedural memory for multi-agent LLM workflow automation; decomposes trajectories into reusable units distributed across orchestrator/agents. Headline: +12.61/+12.72/+13.38pp absolute on LLM/Hybrid/SLM teams over memory-less baseline; baselines: no-memory, Synapse, Agent Workflow Memory (AWM); primary dataset: OfficeBench (300 multi-step tasks across Word, Excel, Calendar, Email, System, OCR-PDF).

Sensory/short-term/long-term human-cognition memory system with offline sleep-time consolidation. Headline: QA accuracy +2.09-6.40% (GPT) and up to +7.67% (Qwen) while reducing token usage 38x/21.8x and API calls 30x/55.5x; baselines: LangMem, A-MEM, MemoryOS, Mem0, NaiveRAG, FullText; primary datasets: LongMemEval (LongMemEval-S) and LoCoMo.

ACT-R cognitive-architecture-grounded LLM agents combining symbolic working memory with LLM reasoning. Headline: improved task performance and grounded decision-making over LLM-only chain-of-thought baselines (no specific headline percent in abstract); baseline: LLM-only chain-of-thought; primary dataset: Design for Manufacturing tasks.

Decoder-only Transformer with auxiliary memory module via cross-attention and gating; preserves general-purpose capabilities. Headline: +86.3% over Llama-3.2 average across BABILong; +37.1% over RMT; +5.0% on MMLU; baselines: vanilla Llama-3.2 and Recurrent Memory Transformer (RMT); primary datasets: BABILong (0K-128K tokens) and MMLU.

Frozen backbone LLM + adaptive residual side-network as memory retriever/reader; ~65K token memory bank. Headline: 40.5% identification accuracy on ChapterBreak vs. GPT-3 28% with 313x fewer parameters; -1.38 to -1.62 perplexity on PG-22; baselines: Memorizing Transformer (MemTRM) and reproduced GPT-2 (407M Alibi); primary datasets: ChapterBreak, PG-22, ArXiv papers, plus ICL on SST-2/MR/Subj/SST-5/MPQA.

Real-time generative agents with option-action framework, asynchronous self-monitoring, summarize-and-forget memory; LyfeGame 3D virtual environment. Headline: 10-100x lower computational cost ($0.50/agent-human-hour vs. ~$25 for Park et al. Generative Agents); 60%+ correct-suspect identification on 9-agent Murder Mystery within 15 simulated minutes; baseline: Park et al. (2023) Stanford Generative Agents; primary dataset: LyfeGame Murder Mystery, Activity Fair, Medicine scenarios.

Long-term memory mechanism with co-trained retriever extending MemoryLLM via 1B-param latent-space memory pool compressing past info into hidden states. Headline: extends knowledge retention from <20K to >160K tokens at similar GPU memory; baselines: MemoryLLM-7B and Llama-3.1-8B with 16K/128K fixed contexts; primary datasets: LongBook-QA (351 book-question pairs averaging 192K tokens) and authors' LongBook Event QA.

Multi-graph memory for long-horizon reasoning across semantic/temporal/causal/entity graphs; policy-guided traversal; ACL 2026. Headline: 0.700 overall on LoCoMo, vs. Full-Context 0.481, A-MEM 0.580, MemoryOS 0.553, Nemori 0.590 (18.6%-45.5% relative gains); baselines: A-MEM, Nemori, MemoryOS, Full-Context; primary datasets: LoCoMo (~9K-token convs) and LongMemEval (100K+ token, 1,986 samples across 5 query categories).

Memory-augmented retriever-reader producing generated text from compact memory. Headline: 56.0 EM on TriviaQA fine-tuned with 100x throughput vs. FiD; baselines: FiD (65 EM, 2.2 q/s) and EMAT (44.4 EM, 190 q/s); primary datasets: TriviaQA, Natural Questions, WebQuestions.

RL framework for agents with core/episodic/semantic memory; trained to decide what to store/structure/update. Headline: 0.642 average on validation, vs. RAG-Top2 0.567 and Long-Context 0.588; generalizes from 30K training to 400K+ tokens (13x training length); baselines: RAG-Top2, Long-Context, MemAgent, MEM1; primary dataset: MemoryAgentBench (Accurate Retrieval, Test-Time Learning, Long-Range Understanding across 9 datasets).

RL framework for constant memory across long multi-turn interactions via shared internal state. Headline: MEM1-7B improves performance 3.5x and reduces memory usage 3.7x vs. Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct on 16-objective multi-hop QA; baselines: Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct, Search-R1, DeepResearcher, Agent-R, AgentLM; primary datasets: multi-objective QA (HotpotQA + NQ composition), WebShop, Wikipedia RAG, Online Web-QA.

Agent workflow processing long documents in segments with overwrite-based memory; extends DAPO for multi-conversation training. Headline: 8K-trained model extrapolates to 3.5M-token QA with <5% loss; 95%+ on 512K RULER; baselines: Qwen2.5-Instruct (incl. 1M variant), DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen, QwenLong-L1-32B; primary datasets: RULER (HotpotQA QA at 7K-3.5M tokens), needle-in-a-haystack, variable tracking, frequent-words extraction.

Stateful memory-augmented BART for efficient dialogue modeling; separate memory module alongside pre-trained transformer for state/context exchange. Headline: superior efficiency and performance vs. pre-trained Transformer baselines (no specific headline metric in abstract); baseline: vanilla BART and pre-trained Transformer baselines; primary datasets: 3 dialogue datasets and 2 language-modeling datasets.

Memory-based online RL via M-MDP with episodic memory and case-selection policy; no LLM fine-tuning. Headline: 87.88% Pass@3 on GAIA validation, 79.40% on test; +4.7-9.6 absolute pp on OOD tasks; baselines: SOTA training-based methods, CoT+RAG (37.7% F1 on DeepResearcher), DeepResearcher (51.8% avg F1); primary datasets: GAIA val/test, DeepResearcher (7 QA datasets), SimpleQA (4,330 q), HLE (2,500 q).

Meta-evolution of agent memory architecture itself, not just experiential knowledge; EvolveLab self-evolving memory codebase with encode/store/retrieve/manage modules. Headline: SmolAgent and Flash-Searcher gains up to 17.06%; baselines: SmolAgent and Flash-Searcher frameworks; primary datasets: 4 challenging agentic benchmarks.

External dynamic memory transformer for sequence modeling; memory replay back-propagation (MRBP) for long-range BPTT. Headline: 8.1x less memory and 3.2x faster inference vs. standard Transformers with comparable performance; baseline: standard Transformers; primary dataset: not specified in abstract (long-sequence language modeling).

Memory-augmented transformers can implement linear first-order optimization (gradient descent, conjugate gradient, momentum); MoE adapts learned methods to OOD scenarios. Headline: competitive with classical CGD/Nesterov AGM/momentum on synthetic linear regression (no specific headline metric); baselines: Conjugate Gradient Descent, Nesterov Accelerated Gradient, Momentum Gradient Descent; primary dataset: synthetic random linear regression (d=5, n=20, Gaussian inputs).

Generative latent memory framework for self-evolving agents with Memory Trigger and Memory Weaver; spontaneously develops planning/procedural/working memory faculties. Headline: surpasses ExpeL and AWM by up to 38.22%; baselines: ExpeL and AWM; primary dataset: 8 benchmarks.

Intent-driven memory selection: intent-aligned retrieval + missing-slot guided filtering with LLaMA-8B re-ranker. Headline: 88%->99% task success and -2.84 turns dialogue length; baselines: Full-context prompting (LLaMA3-8B, Qwen2.5-7B, Mistral-7B, GPT-4o-mini), BERT-DST, LDST, AutoTOD, ChatCite; primary dataset: MS-TOD (132 personas, 956 task goals, 2,861 dialogues across 16 domains).
